title: mcp-bin configuration
description: Configuration file for mcp-bin. Defines MCP servers to connect to and files/directories to embed in compiled binaries. Supports JSON (.json) and YAML (.yaml/.yml) formats.

files.description: File and directory paths to embed in compiled binaries. Directories are recursively included. At runtime, embedded content is extracted to a cache directory (~/.cache/mcp-bin/<hash>/dirs/) and paths in the config are automatically patched to point to the extracted location.

items.description: Relative or absolute path to a file or directory to embed.

servers.description: Map of server names to their configurations. Each key becomes a CLI subcommand name (e.g., 'my-server' becomes './mcp-bin my-server <tool>').

serverConfig.description: Configuration for a single MCP server. Must specify either 'command' for local stdio-based servers or 'url' for remote HTTP-based servers, but not both.

description.description: Human-readable description of the server, shown in --help output and skill documents.

command.description: Executable command to start a local MCP server using stdio transport. The server process is spawned as a child process and communicates via stdin/stdout.

args.description: Command-line arguments passed to the server command.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the server process. Values support ${VAR} syntax for substitution from the host environment. In compiled binaries, the original variable name is preserved so it can be overridden at runtime.

cwd.description: Working directory for the server process. In compiled binaries, this path is automatically patched to point to the extracted cache location if it matches an embedded file path.

url.description: URL endpoint for a remote MCP server. The client first attempts Streamable HTTP transport, then falls back to SSE (Server-Sent Events) if that fails.

headers.description: HTTP headers sent with requests to remote servers. Commonly used for authentication (e.g., 'Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}'). Values support ${VAR} syntax for environment variable substitution.

allow_tools.description: Whitelist of tool name patterns. When set, only tools whose names match at least one pattern are visible as CLI commands. Supports glob patterns with '*' (e.g., 'read_*' matches 'read_file', 'read_url'). Applied before deny_tools. When omitted, all tools are visible by default.

deny_tools.description: Blacklist of tool name patterns. Tools whose names match any pattern are hidden from CLI commands. Supports glob patterns with '*'. Applied after allow_tools filtering, so it can exclude specific tools from an allowed set. When omitted, no tools are denied.

oauth.description: OAuth2 configuration. Endpoints are auto-discovered from the server URL via RFC 9728/8414. Only client credentials and scopes need to be specified when required by the provider.

client_id.description: OAuth2 client ID. Supports ${VAR} env var substitution. Optional if the server supports dynamic client registration.

client_secret.description: OAuth2 client secret. Supports ${VAR} env var substitution. Optional for public clients.

scopes.description: OAuth2 scopes to request. If omitted, uses server-advertised defaults.
